extraction.one_line_summary: CARAPAZ won two-up sprint after 28-rider breakaway held, taking stage while IGITA won race lead by 16 seconds through cooperation pact.
tactical_patterns: UAE and Ineos cooperated in 28-rider break, suggesting pre-race arrangement where Carapaz takes stage, Igita takes GC lead.
Jumbo-Visma abandoned chase after Soler consultation at team car, revealing unwillingness to work for rivals' interests mid-stage.
AYUSO isolated without race radio attacked unaware of small gap behind, showing communication failure cost bridging opportunity to leaders.
Only Jumbo briefly chased when UAE tired, but peloton refused cooperation allowing 28-rider group to hold minute with 80km remaining.

rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: Won sprint from wheel after full cooperation, showing superior tactics and finishing speed despite equal workload
rider_signals.IGITA Juan Pedro: neutral: Lost stage sprint from front but achieved primary GC objective, started sprint too early at 100m out
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: negative: Bridged to leaders solo but lacked race radio awareness, caught by chase group due to poor communication
rider_signals.QUINTANA Naiqui: negative: Lost race lead by failing to make 28-rider move, relegated to chasing group showing diminished climbing form
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: negative: Finished minute down after UAE worked for breakaway instead of defending his GC, poor team tactical coordination
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: negative: Abandoned work after team car consultation despite being in position to chase, showed lack of commitment
